statement.p: The formula for a cylinders volume is V=\pi r^2h\text{,} where V is the volume, r is the base radius, and h is the height. And \pi is a number that is about 3.14\text{.} If we know the volume of a cylinder is {{\frac{3}{5}}\pi } and if we also know its height is {{\frac{5}{3}}} then we have the equation {{\frac{3}{5}}\pi }={\pi r^{2}{\frac{5}{3}}}\text{.} Is the radius {{\frac{4}{5}}}\text{?}
